root_cause: OCS cognition and semantic resolution surfaced ambiguity but lacked a dedicated replay-visible clarification artifact that normalized requests and preserved continuity and semantic references.
runtime_model: OCS clarification consumes explicit OCS cognition and semantic resolution artifacts.
The runtime validates replay visibility, artifact hashes, known OCS artifact types, known OCS statuses, and semantic-to-cognition lineage hash continuity.
The runtime rejects any source carrying authority-bearing flags.
The runtime converts required cognition clarification requirements and semantic clarification candidates into deterministic clarification requests.
The runtime preserves semantic references and continuity references from semantic resolution.
The runtime records both clarification-required and clarification-not-required states.
The runtime marks all outputs as replay-visible and non-authoritative.
The clarification hash is derived from source hashes, clarification requests, ambiguity evidence, continuity references, semantic references, authority flags, status, and failure state.
Replay reconstruction validates ordering, wrapper hashes, artifact hashes, returned continuity, and clarification hash continuity.
replay_impact: OCS now has append-only replay-visible clarification evidence.
Replay reconstruction can prove which cognition and semantic resolution artifacts produced the clarification requests.
Identical ambiguity inputs produce the same clarification hash.
The runtime does not mutate source replay.
The runtime does not invoke PPP, providers, workers, approval, governance mutation, implementation, or execution.
